FACTS
You represent a city with an exj.sting law requirilg payment
of a busineis 1icense te- Uy aII Iessors of residential rental
,rrrit=. The fee is calculated on a per-unit basis and apPlies to
all rental units, including single rental units Iocated behind
orr.,".-o"".rpied residences on n-i lots. 
The city council. is
-onsiaer:.n| anending the law to exempt fron the fee requirernent
in" iir=t iental unit on each property owned by persons ovrning no
nore than five rental units in the city.
The city is zoned Prinarily, i.e., eighty percent, for R-2
property ,sage. Ay youi estimation in a recent telephone
'.or,?..".,"., ipproxinat"Iy fifty percent of the residential
properties in-tne city have a rental' unit located thereon'
Three city councilnembers own one rental unit each in the
city, and each of those councilmembers is concerned about
participation in the fee exernption decision'

section 8710o prohibits public officials 
from making,

The Act seeks to prevent

tt. upp....nce of a possible conflict of interest'

ltorrorr, supra, at 823.)

foreseeable that there wiit ne a f i'nancial- effect on their

rental business interests frorn the decision'
consequently, none ;; ah" three nay participate in the decision if
ttr"-.ii."t 
6n their properties is irateriat, unless the effect is
inai=i ing"j.shable rrtm tne effect on the public generally'
Materia I itv
Regulation La702-2 (copy enclosed). sets forth the standards
for deternining mateiiiritv'iith 
."rp."t to financial interests in
l.rsin".="= indirectly affeltea by governmenta I' decisions'
For a relatively snall business, the effect of a decision is
naterial if any of the fo)'lowing applies:
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
of S1o,000 or nore; or
(2) The decision wiII resul-t in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reducing or eliminating existing expenses for a
fiscal year in the amount of 52,500 or more,' or
(3) The decision wj'II result in an increase
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of $1o,0oo or more.
section r8702.2 (s) (1) (2) (3)
Unless the proPerty rental businesses of each of the
councilmembers who 6wns- a rental unit is affected by the fee
exemption decision in at least one of the ways set. forth above'
the iinancial affect would not be considered rnaterial, and the
suUject councilmembers would not be required to disqualify
theirselves from participation in that decision'

even if a decisi6n wii.l have a reasonably foreseeable rnaterial
"tf."t 
on an official's 
econonic interest, the official 
nay.
piiiicipat" 
in the decision if the effect on his interest will be
substantiatly the sane as the effect on the publi'c generally'
For the "public generally" exception to apply in the
circunstances iou havi described, a decision nust affect an
oiii"iul's 
intlrest in substantially the same manner as it would

a significant 
segTment of the persons residing or doinq

in the city.

FPPC ops. 77 (coPY enc)-osed) . )
The facts provided indicate that fifty percent of.the

owners of one-half of aII of the residential property in the city
,""ia-""*p.ise 
a significant segment of the total nunber of
residential property owners. since the decision involves a per
unit tax asslssment. the rental property interests of each
official 
who has one rental unit on his or her property would be
affectedinSubstantiallytheSamenanner.Inthiscircumstance,
ine "puff i.c generallyrr eiception would be applicable'

ino="'officiils

fiom participation in the fee exemptj'on decision'
